channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pentagon orders new purge of social media sites to dump diversity, inclusion mentions by March 5
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Building lethality in the military may be the buzzword for the new Trump administration, but busywork and paperwork have become the reality at the Pentagon, as service members and civilian workers are facing a broad mandate to purge all of the department's social media sites and un
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VA pauses billions in cuts lauded by Musk as lawmakers and veterans decry loss of critical care
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs has temporarily suspended billions of dollars in contract cuts after an uproar that critical veterans health services were harmed, lawmakers and veterans service organizations said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration sets March 21 deadline for New York to end traffic congestion pricing
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has given New York until March 21 to comply with its order to halt Manhattan’s new congestion pricing system.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dem bid to end Trump energy emergency squashed after forced Senate vote
item.description: Democrats in the Senate failed to pass a resolution that would have reversed President Donald Trump’s executive order on "Unleashing American Energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa Bay area families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National parks already feel the effects of layoffs, even before the busy season starts
item.description: Some 1,000 NPS employees were fired, and hiring for seasonal positions was delayed. Here’s what to know about the impacts already being felt at parks, and what it could mean for the busy season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans barrel toward showdown over Trump tax cuts after dramatic House budget vote
item.description: House and Senate Republicans are at odds over how to go about extending President Trump's tax cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Democrats Cortez Masto, Rosen join Republicans in scrubbing Biden-era drilling rule
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen joined their Republican colleagues in voting to reverse a Biden-era policy related to offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor of America’s biggest city thanks Trump for crackdown on criminal immigrants
item.description: Mayor Eric Adams of New York thanked the Trump administration for cracking down on criminal illegal immigrants, which helped reduce the stream of immigrants in the city by over 1,000%.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge hints she will continue blocking Trump from firing head of whistleblower protection agency
item.description: A federal judge indicated she will extend a temporary restraining order on President Donald Trump's effort to fire Office of Special Counsel head Hampton Dellinger.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump said that he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US lines up Latin American cooperation for migrant deportations
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Venezuelan migrants handed over to Mexico like it’s a U.S. immigration detention facility. Families from Central Asia flown to Panama and Costa Rica to await voluntary repatriation to their countries.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrat introduces bill to reinstate veterans fired from the federal government under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A freshman Democratic congressman is introducing a bill to protect the jobs of veterans working for the U.S. government amid mass firings by the Trump administration, the latest legislative response to the turmoil rippling across federal agencies. The bill from Rep.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams skips forum as he awaits decision on legal fate
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he pulled out of a mayoral candidate forum after his lawyer told him to wait until a federal judge decides how to handle the corruption case.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump introduces a green card for the rich: the gold card
item.description: The new visa program would effectively replace the current EB-5 investor visa program, and the proceeds could be used for deficit reduction.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Erik Prince: Private sector can help with mass deportations
item.description: "It was a memo generated to describe how to achieve the logistics necessary to move the millions of people that they intend to deport," Prince said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration creates registry for immigrants who are in the US illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is creating a registry for all people who are in the United States illegally, and those who don't self-report could face fines or prosecution, immigration officials announced Tuesday. Everyone who is in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEIU members demand better contract at Kern County Board of Supervisors meeting
item.description: The union is set to hold a one-day strike March 5, in hopes of bringing Kern County negotiators back to the bargaining table.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon as the U.S. government's human resources agency considers how to fulfill Elon Musk ’s demands.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illegal marijuana taking a big bite out of Nevada tax revenue, school funding
item.description: The illicit market for cannabis in Nevada has grown to a $242 million business, taking a bite out of the state's effort to profit from the legalization of marijuana.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shutdown fight collides with GOP push for Trump priorities
item.description: The race to prevent a government shutdown next month is colliding with Republican efforts to enact President Trump’s tax and funding priorities. Congressional negotiators have been struggling for weeks to strike a bipartisan deal that would keep the government funded past a mid-March deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How federal funding flows from the White House to Minnesotans
item.description: Amid the Trump administration’s slashes to program budgets from scientific research to education to social services, nonprofits and state agencies are left in financial and legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellen Finn, MPR News

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans have doubts about whether House budget bill can get votes
item.description: “There’s probably 10 (Republicans) that are really nervous about the situation," one representative said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5
